rulings.Case Title: MARY HATCH VS. RODNEY CELLA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Deft Rodney Cella'S Motoin For Order Compelling Joinder Of Indispensable Parties, Or In The Alternative, Dismissal Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 3, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANT RODNEY CELLA Notice Of Defendant Rodney Cella'S Motion For Order Compelling Joinder Of Indispensable Parties, Or In The Alternative, Dismissal Of Action DENIED. As joinder is not feasible, defendant's motion for order compelling joinder of indispensable parties is DENIED. The trial court can address issues of what damages plaintiff may seek. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BALANCED SUCCESS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. CITADEL RE & INVESTMENTS, LLC,A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 3, 2015, line 6. DEFENDANT ALAIN VU, CITADEL RE & INVESTMENTS LLC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Plaintiff's complaint is adequately pleaded. The demurrer disputes facts, which is not its proper function. Defendants to answer within 10 days of notice of entry of order. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: AMY SHU YI ZHANG VS. DUNG PHU THI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 3, 2015, line 11. DEFENDANT VI TAY THI Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden under CCP 473(b). = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: AMY SHU YI ZHANG VS. DUNG PHU THI ET AL

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BRENNAN COMPANY LLC VS. BARBARA HAMPTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday November 3, 2015, line 8. DEFENDANT BARBARA HAMPTON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint for Unlawful Detainer is not uncertain within the meaning of CCP 430.10(f). Defendant's counsel is reminded again that failure to comply with SFLR 2.6B may result in the motion being taken off calendar. = (501/RBU)
